summary: Refactored the Rust `tools/config-dtk` tool by moving CodeWarrior map parsing logic out of `main.rs` into a new `cw_map.rs` module. The PR appears to be almost entirely a file split: `main.rs` shrank from containing the parser implementation to importing `mod cw_map;` and using `cw_map::Context`, while `cw_map.rs` now owns section-name conversion, identifier formatting, parsed symbol structs, split/section tracking, source path mapping, and line matching over `cwparse` map/table/tree data.

key_files.change: Removed the bulk of CodeWarrior map parsing implementation and replaced it with `mod cw_map;` plus imports of `cw_map::Context`; retained main/config output responsibilities.
New module containing the moved map parsing context, symbol structs, section/id formatting helpers, split and section tracking, source path handling, and cwparse line matching logic.

what_changed: Added `tools/config-dtk/src/cw_map.rs` with roughly 501 lines of map parsing code formerly embedded in `main.rs`.
Reduced `tools/config-dtk/src/main.rs` by roughly 497 deleted lines, leaving it to import and use `cw_map::Context` rather than define the parser inline.
Made parser-facing types and fields visible within the crate where needed: `Context`, `TreeSymbol`, `TableSymbol`, and selected fields are marked `pub(crate)` in the new module.

Kept parser behavior visible in the diff largely intact: map lines are parsed in parallel with `par_lines`, symbols are stored in `AHashMap`s keyed by hashed translation unit plus identifier, and errors/warnings preserve contextual raw-line formatting.

Preserved CodeWarrior section and identifier normalization helpers, including names such as `.text`, `.data`, `.bss`, `.rodata`, `extab`, `extabindex`, `@stringBase{idx}`, `lbl{name}`, and section-derived labels.
smart_moves: Separated a large parser subsystem from the executable entry point, making `main.rs` easier to scan and reducing coupling between CLI/config output code and CodeWarrior map parsing internals.
Used a crate-private module boundary instead of making the parser API fully public, exposing only what `main.rs` needs through `pub(crate)`.
Preserved the existing parser context shape rather than combining the refactor with semantic changes, which lowers review risk for a tooling cleanup.
Kept detailed parser diagnostics close to the parser state via `format_error_raw` and `format_error`, maintaining line/raw text context after the module split.
Maintained existing performance-oriented parsing structure using Rayon parallel line parsing and `AHashMap`/`RandomState` hashing.
decomp_lessons: For decomp support tools, split large parsing logic out of `main.rs` once it grows into its own domain model; this makes future config-generation changes less likely to accidentally disturb parser behavior.
When moving code into a Rust module, prefer `pub(crate)` for structs and fields consumed by sibling modules instead of widening visibility to `pub` without need.
Refactors of build/config tools should be kept behavior-preserving when possible: a direct module extraction is easier to validate than a file split mixed with parser rewrites.
Keep CodeWarrior map normalization rules centralized; section names, symbol identifier formatting, TU stripping, and split tracking are reusable concepts for future DTK/config tooling.
Parser diagnostics that include source line number and raw line text are useful for maintaining decomp tooling that consumes compiler/linker artifacts with edge cases.
naming_conventions: `cw_map` was chosen as the module name for CodeWarrior map parsing responsibilities.
The parser context type remains named `Context`, imported as `cw_map::Context` in `main.rs`.
Rust raw identifiers are used for reserved words such as `r#type` in structs that model symbol or section type fields.

CodeWarrior section names are normalized to output strings including `.bss`, `.ctors`, `.data`, `.init`, `.rodata`, `.sbss`, `.sbss2`, `.sdata`, `.sdata2`, `.text`, `.dtors`, plus `extab` and `extabindex` without leading dots as shown in the diff.

Identifier formatting conventions preserved include relative symbols as `@{idx}`, string-base symbols as `@stringBase{idx}`, duplicate named instances as `name$instance`, section indexed identifiers as `..section.idx`, and DotL labels as `lbl{name}`.
Translation unit names are normalized by stripping a trailing `.o` via `strip_tu`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR is tooling-focused and does not directly change decompiled C/assembly matching.

The moved parser preserves tactics useful to config generation: detect split starts from section symbols whose object name ends with `.c.o`, close the previous split when a new one begins, and track section start/end addresses as `NonZeroU32`.
Symbol lookup keys are built by hashing the translation unit and CodeWarrior map identifier together, reducing collisions between same-named symbols from different object files.
Source paths are filtered to `.c` files and stored relative to the project root using `pathdiff::diff_paths`, which supports generating stable config paths.
Zero-size symbols or sections are not silently ignored everywhere: the parser emits warnings/debug messages, which helps diagnose map anomalies during config work.

review_feedback.feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs modifying `tools/config-dtk/src/cw_map.rs` to see whether the module split enabled parser fixes or API changes.
Search for changes to `cw_map::Context` consumers in `tools/config-dtk/src/main.rs` after PR 1084.
Look for PRs touching `cwparse` handling, section normalization, or split detection for `.c.o` objects.
Check whether later review feedback requested further splitting of `cw_map.rs` into symbol, section, and source-path submodules.
